item.title: Frequency Domain Muon for Convolutional Neural Networks: Simplified
item.description: The 'correct' way to apply Muon to CNNs

item.title: LUCID-MoE: Mixture of Experts with Preconditioned Routing
item.description: Sharper Mixture-of-Experts routing with LUCID preconditioning.

item.title: Error-Compensating Optimizers: ECO-AdamW, ECO-Muon, and Beyond
item.description: Quantized training without full-precision master weights, extended to handle weight decay and matrix LMOs.

item.title: Bidirectional-PRISM: Kronecker-Factored Optimization via Anisotropic Spectral Shaping
item.description: A novel optimizer that combines Shampoo-style preconditioning with PRISM's anisotropic spectral shaping to adaptively suppress noisy gradient directions while maximally descending under the spectral norm trust-region constraint.

item.title: Steepest Descent on Affine-Conic Representable Manifolds with Boundary via Dual Ascent
item.description: Novel optimizers for maximally descending on the loss landscape while satisfying strict weight constraints.

item.title: Steepest Descent on the Birkhoff Polytope Equipped with the Spectral Norm
item.description: We derive an optimizer that performs steepest descent on the Birkhoff polytope equipped with the spectral norm via dual ascent. We show that it yields larger effective weight updates than naive LMO-based optimizers.

item.title: Sensitivity and Sharpness of Gated Linear Attention Mechanisms
item.description: We derive sensitivity and sharpness bounds for Gated DeltaNet and Mamba 2, showing that they can be made 1-Lipschitz with appropriate parameter constraints.

item.title: Convergence Bounds for Steepest Descent Under Arbitrary Norms
item.description: First-order optimization under arbitrary norms with Nesterov momentum (and decoupled weight decay) yields a universal convergence bound. Our results generalize to norms not induced by inner products, and also considers batch size.

item.title: Critical Batch Size for Steepest Descent Under Arbitrary Norms
item.description: First-order optimization under arbitrary norms with Nesterov momentum (and decoupled weight decay) yields universal critical batch size scaling laws. Under an additional local-LMO assumption, the same analysis also heuristically supports square-root learning-rate scaling with batch size.

item.title: Rethinking Maximal Update Parametrization: Steepest Descent on Finsler-Structured (Matrix) Geometries via Dual Ascent
item.description: To guarantee fast and robust model training, we can recast the optimization problem as steepest descent on Finsler-structured geometries. Here we show how to compute the optimal updates via dual ascent.

item.title: Rethinking Maximal Update Parametrization: Steepest Descent on the Spectral Ball
item.description: Novel optimizers for maximally updating both the weights and activations of neural networks while keeping weight norms under control. To get there, we needed to invent an efficient, GPU/TPU-friendly method for eigenvalue clipping and solve the Steepest Descent problem on the Positive Semidefinite Cone, Convex Spectrahedron, and finally on the Spectral Ball.

item.title: Factorization-free Eigenvalue Clipping and Steepest Descent on the Positive Semidefinite Cone, Convex Spectrahedron, and Spectral Ball
item.description: Novel optimizers for maximally updating both the weights and activations of neural networks while keeping weight norms under control. To get there, we needed to invent a cheap, GPU/TPU-friendly method for eigenvalue clipping and steepest descent on the positive semidefinite cone, convex spectrahedron, and finally steepest descent on the spectral ball.

item.title: Steepest Descent on Finsler-Structured (Matrix) Manifolds
item.description: Fast and robust model training.

item.title: Heuristic Solutions for Steepest Descent on the Stiefel Manifold
item.description: What would Muon look like if we constrained the weights to be semi-orthogonal?

item.title: Sensitivity and Sharpness of n-Simplicial Attention
item.description: Towards a maximal update parameterization of n-simplicial attention

item.title: Adam with Aggressive Gradient Clipping ≈ Smoothed SignSGD/NormSGD
item.description: Why does Adam with aggressive gradient value/norm clipping have sparse updates and do well with higher learning rates? Here we show that it asymptotically matches Smoothed SignSGD in the value-clipping limit and tracks a rescaled Smoothed NormSGD direction in the norm-clipping limit.

item.title: Fast, Numerically Stable, and Auto-Differentiable Spectral Clipping via Newton-Schulz Iteration
item.description: A small step towards hardware-architecture-optimizer codesign in deep learning.

item.title: Muon and a Selective Survey on Steepest Descent in Riemannian and Non-Riemannian Manifolds
item.description: Muon from first principles, what makes it different from other optimizers, and why it works so well.

item.title: Napkin Math on Non-Euclidean Trust Region Optimization
item.description: A possible reason why Muon converges faster & does better at higher learning rates than Adam.

item.title: Block Matrix Formulation of Linear Attention Mechanisms
item.description: The block matrix formulation of linear attention mechanisms, multi-step online gradient descent at inference time, and chunk-wise parallelism.

item.title: Steepest Descent Under Schatten-p Norms
item.description: Why Muon still work despite not perfectly semi-orthogonalizing the gradients.

item.title: Squeezing 1-2% Efficiency Gains Out of Muon by Optimizing the Newton-Schulz Coefficients
item.description: Simply switching to Muon can already get you 2x efficiency gains. But you can squeeze out an extra 1-2% by optimizing the Newton-Schulz coefficients.

item.title: CASPR Without Accumulation is Muon
item.description: The CASPR optimizer, a variant of Shampoo, reduces to Muon when we remove the accumulation on the preconditioners.

item.title: GRPO's Main Flaw
item.description: GRPO may not be the best choice for training reasoning models. Here's why.

item.title: (Linear) Attention as Test-Time Regression
item.description: A unifying framework for linear attention mechanisms as test-time regression and how to parallelize training and inference.

item.title: Deep Learning Optimizers as Steepest Descent in Normed Spaces
item.description: Instead of asking, 'Which optimizer should I use?' ask, 'In which space do my features live in?'

item.title: ChatGPT May Have Developed Seasonal Depression
item.description: Could ChatGPT's shorter responses be an indication of something more bizarre going on?

item.title: The Human Mind May Be Universal
item.description: Years of experience in building artificial minds led me to believe that these AIs may end up seeming more 'human' than we currently imagine them to be.

item.title: Four Rules for Rulers
item.description: On how to gain and maintain power.

item.title: Vaccine Search as a Computational Problem
item.description: A thought dump on mRNA vaccines and the future of computational biology

item.title: The Accuracy-Fairness Dilemma in Machine Learning
item.description: Machine learning models merely amplify our biases - not eliminate them.

item.title: How to Master Machine Learning: 3 Tips to Get Started
item.description: Whether you're only here for the hype or genuinely interested in the field, you’re in for a wild ride.
